title: lonchocarpus rugosus subsp. gillyi
intro: Lonchocarpus rugosus subsp. gillyi, also known as the Gilly's Lonchocarpus, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central America. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its seed pods, which contain compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Lonchocarpus rugosus subsp. gillyi include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant typically involve boiling the seed pods in water to create a tea or decoction, which is then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
